title: Australia Individual Tax Return (myTax, 2026 income year)
description: Prepare and lodge an individual's Australian income tax return online using myTax, the Australian Taxation Office's myGov-linked online lodgment service, for the 2026 income year (1 July 2025 to 30 June 2026). myTax pre-fills most return data from employers, banks, government agencies, health funds and other third parties by late July each year; the individual reviews the pre-filled figures, amends or completes any that are missing or wrong, and lodges. This document models the field set of the ATO's paper equivalent, the 'Tax return for individuals' (NAT 2541), which myTax's online screens mirror: personal and contact details, electronic funds transfer details, income items 1-12 (salary and wages, allowances, employer lump sums, employment termination payments, government allowances and pensions, annuities and superannuation income streams, superannuation lump sums, attributed personal services income, interest, dividends, and employee share schemes), deductions D1-D10 and prior-year losses, tax offsets T1-T2, Medicare levy questions M1-M2 and private health insurance policy details, adjustments A1-A4, income tests IT1-IT8, spouse details for taxpayers with a spouse (married or de facto) during the year, and the taxpayer's declaration. Out of scope: the separate 'Tax return for individuals (supplementary section) 2026' (business income and expenses, capital gains, foreign income and assets, partnership and trust distributions, rental schedules, and other less-common income/deductions), gated by this document's requiresSupplementarySection field but not itself modelled here; multiple concurrent employers (the source form's five repeating salary/wage payer rows) and multiple concurrent private health insurance policies (the source form's four repeating policy rows), both of which are repeating third-party structures the current flat field model (GSP-0009) cannot express; and the transfer of a refund to a different taxpayer's income tax account or student loan. It does not submit the return or calculate tax payable; the live ATO myTax service and the live NAT 2541 source are always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md).

source.documentRef: ATO 'Lodge your tax return online with myTax' guidance page, plus the official 'Tax return for individuals 2026' (NAT 2541-06.2026) paper form whose field set myTax's online screens mirror

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored from two primary sources. (1) The ATO's 'Lodge your tax return online with myTax' page (fetched via a recent Wayback Machine capture, 2026-06-16, after the live ato.gov.au domain returned HTTP 403 to both a direct curl fetch and WebFetch in this sandbox — add ato.gov.au to the WAF/bot-protection-blocked domain list alongside nzta.govt.nz and canada.ca; the Wayback capture was fetched with curl --compressed since the response was gzip-encoded), which supplied the myGov-account-linkage eligibility gate, the pre-fill description (employers, banks, government agencies, health funds and third parties by late July; myDeductions uploads), the 31 October lodgment due date, and the 'Manage tax returns' access path. (2) The official 'Tax return for individuals 2026' (NAT 2541-06.2026) PDF, downloaded directly from iorder.com.au (the ATO's Publications Ordering Service), which supplied every numbered field: this is a static (non-fillable) PDF with no /AcroForm or /XFA marker (getFieldObjects() returns null), the same shape as NZTA's DL1 form, so fields were reconstructed from the pdfjs-dist text layer's x/y position data (items sorted by y descending then x ascending; each numbered item/question's boxes and letter codes read off from their printed position) rather than AcroForm field names. Every field is traced to the printed item/question number and, where the form itself prints one, the single-letter box code (e.g. item 7's 'J' for the taxed-element box) in sourceRef. Three sourcing caveats are recorded honestly rather than resolved by guessing (see the affected fields' own descriptions): the exact box(es) comprising superLumpSumTaxableComponentUntaxed at the item 8/item 9 boundary; whether 'Litigation costs', printed directly above D10, names a component of D10 or a separate amount; and whether the three boxes below A3 (income from investment/partnership/other sources, other income from employment and business, other deductions from business income) belong to A3 or a separate unlabelled block. This is the first published GovSchema document for the AU 'review a pre-filled return' flow shape shared by the AU myTax, DE ELSTER and SG myTax Portal/Form B1 tax-return candidates: the applicant does not fill a blank form but reviews, amends, and confirms figures the tax authority has already assembled from third-party reporting, which is why every field here is optional unless the source form itself marks it compulsory (e.g. M2, the declaration) — myTax may supply a value the applicant never manually types. Modelled at the plain (non-edition) registry path: the closed edition.scheme vocabulary (spec v0.3 SS5.7: us-tax-year / gb-tax-year / award-year) has no scheme for Australia's 1 July-30 June income year without mislabeling it as one of those other countries' tax years, the same GSP-0019 gap already flagged for ie/revenue/self-assessment-tax-return-form11s and nz/ird/individual-tax-return-ir3 (now confirmed in a fourth jurisdiction) — see spec/proposals/0019-generalize-edition-scheme-calendar-tax-year.md. No live myTax walkthrough was performed (myTax requires an authenticated, linked myGov account not available in this environment); VERIFICATION.md includes a worked mock-data walkthrough instead, and the conformance packet validates a complete example application against this schema's own required/requiredWhen constraints. Status remains 'draft'. See VERIFICATION.md.

documents.label: Taxpayer's declaration
documents.statement: I declare that: all the information I have given on this tax return, including any attachments, is true and correct; I have shown all my income, including net capital gains, for tax purposes for 2025-26; I have completed and attached the supplementary section, schedules and other attachments, as appropriate, that the instructions told me to provide; I have completed question M2 - Medicare levy surcharge; and I have the necessary receipts and/or other records, or expect to obtain the necessary written evidence within a reasonable time of lodging this tax return, to support my claims for deductions and tax offsets.

fields.name: hasMyGovAccountLinkedToAto
fields.label: myGov account with an active link to the ATO
fields.description: You need a myGov account with an active link to the ATO to access online services and lodge with myTax. Sign in to myGov, select ATO from your linked services, then select 'Manage tax returns'.
fields.sourceRef: ato.gov.au 'Lodge your tax return online with myTax' — 'Access to myTax'

fields.label: For the whole of 2025-26, were you a single person with income for surcharge purposes of $101,000 or less, or a member of a family with combined income of $202,000 or less (plus $1,500 per dependent child after the first), and were all dependants in a Medicare levy exemption category?

fields.name: medicareLevySurchargeExemptDays
fields.label: Number of days you don't have to pay the Medicare levy surcharge
fields.description: 0 if you must pay the surcharge for the whole period; 365 if you don't have to pay it for the whole period; otherwise the number of days for part of the period.

fields.label: Do you consent to use part or all of your 2026 tax refund to repay your spouse's Family Assistance debt?

fields.name: spouseCustomerReferenceNumber
fields.label: Your spouse's Centrelink Customer Reference Number (CRN)
fields.description: Only if you consent to use your refund to repay your spouse's Family Assistance debt, and your spouse has given you authority to quote their CRN.

fields.name: familyAssistanceConsentSignatureDate
fields.label: Date you signed the Family Assistance consent

fields.name: requiresSupplementarySection
fields.label: Are you required to complete any of the questions in the Tax return for individuals (supplementary section) 2026?
fields.description: The supplementary section itself (additional business, capital gains, foreign income, rental, and partnership/trust schedules) is out of scope for this document — see VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: furtherInformationAttached
fields.label: Have the instructions asked you to attach further information relating to specific questions?

fields.name: declarationSigned
fields.label: Declaration signed
fields.description: You must read the declaration and sign the return as being true and correct before lodging.

fields.name: declarationDate
fields.label: Date of signature
